summary.intent: Refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching between editing code and viewing Claude Code sessions, then iteratively build out its features including checkpoints sidebar, search, MCP server, image rendering, and onboarding flow.
summary.outcome: A fully functional VS Code extension was built with sessions list, session detail view, native checkpoint tree sidebar, MCP server embedded at /mcp, search via QuickPick with FTS, image rendering, agent-aware labels and commands, VSIX packaging script, onboarding flow for new users, and a stable port architecture. The extension was packaged and deployable via a shell script.

repo: Each repo gets its own SQLite database at ~/.gossamer/repos/<name>.db; the config file is at ~/.gossamer/config.json
Session data flows: Entire.io CLI → git branch (entire/checkpoints/v1 and shadow branches) → gossamer indexes → SQLite → display
Custom session titles from /rename are stored as custom-title events in ~/.claude/projects/<path>/*.jsonl, NOT in the checkpoint or shadow branches - must be read separately
The slug field on LogEvent is auto-generated and never changes on /rename; custom-title events are the actual rename signal
Images are stored as base64 in full.jsonl files on the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch
The .gossamer/ directory gets recreated inside repos because ensureWorktree() creates it as a git worktree for the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch - add to .gitignore
Session color data is NOT stored in any file - it's in-memory UI state only
Agent name comes from CheckpointSessionMetadata.agent field
The worktree working tree doesn't auto-update when Entire commits to entire/checkpoints/v1 via plumbing - must explicitly git reset --hard before reading files

code.finding: startServer() must call pushSchema() before syncLogContentFts() - fresh DBs have no tables and syncLogContentFts fails with 'no such table: LogContent'
Generated via 'prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema ... --script' with sed to add IF NOT EXISTS - replaces Prisma CLI dependency for schema initialization in bundled VSIX
Server is a static singleton (static server, static serverPort) that survives panel disposal - enables minimize/restore pattern without server restart
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once per webview - must store as window.__vscodeApi and reuse
Must use include: ['src/**/*.ts'] not specific files - listing only extension.ts and GossamerPanel.ts caused SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts to never compile
resourceUri must use absolute paths for SCM decorations to work - relative paths get no color coding
pushSchema SQL parsing bug: splitting on ';' leaves comment lines starting with '--' which were incorrectly filtered, causing all CREATE TABLE statements to be skipped
Must listen to onDidChangeViewState to update checkpoint sidebar when switching between existing tabs - sidebar only updates on panel creation otherwise

workflow: The gossamer-testing git worktree has completely separate file copies - every code change must be synced manually (cp) and rebuilt independently in that directory
VS Code auxiliary/standalone windows don't have a sidebar or activity bar - native TreeView cannot be embedded in them
VS Code webviews reload when dragged to a new window - must re-send server_ready via onDidChangeViewState
VS Code CSP blocks data: URIs for images by default - must add img-src data: https: http: to the Content-Security-Policy header
Bundling Prisma CLI (62MB) into a VSIX is impractical - use pre-generated schema.sql with IF NOT EXISTS statements instead
npx should not be called from VS Code extensions - use pre-generated SQL or bundle the binary properly
Vite builds ESM output - webview script tags need type='module' or browser silently ignores them
Chord keybindings (Ctrl+K G) are safer than Cmd+Shift+letter which conflicts with VS Code core
The server runs from gossamer-testing/dist/serve.js (the worktree), not gossamer/dist/ - must rebuild in the worktree to test changes

friction: The gossamer-testing worktree pattern required manually syncing files on every change - very tedious and error-prone
prisma db push fails inside bundled VSIX because Prisma CLI isn't bundled - required developing schema.sql fallback approach
Schema SQL parsing bug (comment lines starting with '--' filtered out) caused 'no such table' errors that were hard to trace
VS Code's bundled Node.js (Electron) has a different NODE_MODULE_VERSION than system Node.js - caused better-sqlite3 native module mismatch requiring the child process spawn workaround
The server runs from gossamer-testing/dist/ not gossamer/dist/ - caused confusion when changes weren't appearing after rebuild
Accidentally applied changes to the Electron app (app/src/main/index.ts) when user explicitly said this was a VS Code extension - had to revert
Toggle panel keybinding (Ctrl+K G) doesn't fire when webview has focus, making minimize unreliable
Vite uses random hash suffixes in output filenames by default - had to configure deterministic names (assets/index.js) for GossamerPanel to reference them
open_items: Session color data is not available from any Entire.io file - feature cannot be implemented unless Entire adds persistence
The toggle panel (minimize) keybinding doesn't work when webview has focus - needs investigation
If a session is renamed but has no subsequent events (user renames and immediately closes), the new name won't appear until next event
The checkpoint remote onboarding step assumes 'entire enable' CLI command exists - needs validation against actual Entire CLI API
Sessions from other machines don't appear if the checkpoint remote isn't set up - the cross-machine sync story depends on entire configure --checkpoint-remote being used
Branch History deduplication uses time-based filtering (createdAt <= updatedAt + 2min) which is a heuristic - edge cases may exist for sessions with long gaps
The .gossamer/ worktree inside repos should be added to users' .gitignore - no automated way to do this currently
The gossamer.vsix was added to git then removed - should add to .gitignore
bundled-server/ directory should probably be in .gitignore
Schema migrations (ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN) are not handled - only CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is safe for re-application; adding new columns to existing tables requires explicit migration logic
